# Trinity runtime profiles (master spec § 18)

The 3 runtime profiles describe HOW the Genesis Trinity (Pulse · Logic · Oracle) is deployed at any moment. Profiles trade between sovereignty (CPU-only / low-power) and throughput (multi-GPU concurrency / large context).

This doc is regenerated from `profiles/runtime/*.yaml` on every invocation of `scripts/trinity/render-runtime-profiles-md.py`. The same YAMLs drive `sovereign-osctl trinity profile {list,show,switch}` and the inference `start-*.sh` scripts — so the doc, the switcher, and the runtime cannot drift.

## Profile summary

| ID | Name | Hardware compat | System total (W) |
|----|------|-----------------|------------------|
| `ultra-sovereign-efficiency` | Ultra-Sovereign Efficiency | sain-01 | ~150 |
| `high-concurrency-burst` | High-Concurrency Agent Burst | sain-01 | ~1170 |
| `deep-context-synthesis` | Deep Context Synthesis | sain-01 | ~1080 |

## Ultra-Sovereign Efficiency

**ID:** `ultra-sovereign-efficiency`

CPU-focused execution. BitNet-b1.58-3B via bitnet.cpp pinned to
cores 0-7 on CCD0; GPUs in persistence-mode-on-but-throttled state.
Use for: background state monitoring · log auditing · autonomous
maintenance · idle hours.

**Hardware-profile compatibility:** sain-01

**Trinity allocations:**

| Agent | Tier | Target | Core mask | Engine | Model |
|-------|------|--------|-----------|--------|-------|
| `conductor_01` | pulse | cpu | `0-7` | `bitnet.cpp` | `BitNet-b1.58-3B` |

**GPU state:**

- Primary: `persistence-throttled` (invocation: `nvidia-smi -i 0 -pm 1 -ac 5001,675`)
- Secondary: `persistence-throttled` (invocation: `nvidia-smi -i 1 -pm 1 -ac 5001,675`)

**Expected power draw (W):**

- cpu: 45
- gpu_primary: 30
- gpu_secondary: 25
- system_total: ~150

**Observability:**

- Primary metric: `sovereign_os_inference_route_total{tier="pulse"}`
- Expected throughput: 5-12 tok/s

## High-Concurrency Agent Burst

**ID:** `high-concurrency-burst`

Asymmetric load balancing across all three hardware planes:
BitNet-13B on full CCD0+CCD1 (CPU cores 0-11),
Qwen-32B-Ternary on RTX 4090 (host-resident by default; opt-in vfio
sandbox; via vllm-vulkan),
DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B on the large-VRAM oracle GPU (llama.cpp FP16).
Use for: multi-agent code-repo processing · long-running parallel
inference tasks · burst workloads.

RECONCILE NOTE (SDD-993, 2026-07-13): the large-VRAM oracle GPU is the
RTX PRO 6000 96 GB primary/main card (installed) — the DeepSeek-R1-70B-FP16
(88 GB) placement is on it. The RTX 5090 32 GB (~350 W) is the new internal
secondary (Blackwell); the RTX 4090 is a host-resident OcuLink eGPU (opt-in
vfio). Two internal cards run x8/x8. To size any tier to real VRAM use
scripts/operator/generate-runtime-profile.py. See docs/sdd/993-*.md.

**Hardware-profile compatibility:** sain-01

**Trinity allocations:**

| Agent | Tier | Target | Core mask | Engine | Model |
|-------|------|--------|-----------|--------|-------|
| `conductor_01` | pulse | cpu | `0-11` | `bitnet.cpp` | `BitNet-b1.58-13B` |
| `translator_01` | logic | cuda:0 | `—` | `vllm-vulkan` | `Qwen-32B-Ternary-Quant` |
| `deep_reasoner_01` | oracle | cuda:1 | `—` | `llama.cpp` | `DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B-FP16` |

**GPU state:**

- Primary: `full-power` (invocation: `nvidia-smi -i 0 -pm 1 -lgc 1410,1965`)
- Secondary: `full-power` (invocation: `nvidia-smi -i 1 -pm 1`)

**Expected power draw (W):**

- cpu: 170
- gpu_primary: 300
- gpu_secondary: 350
- gpu_egpu: 350
- system_total: ~1170

**Observability:**

- Primary metric: `sovereign_os_inference_route_total`

## Deep Context Synthesis

**ID:** `deep-context-synthesis`

Chains both GPUs into one unified execution space via tensor-parallel.
Uses CPU only for streaming tokenizers (no Pulse). KV cache compressed
to fp8 to maximize active context length.
Use for: whole-application source parsing · telemetry-wide reasoning ·
long-context architectural analysis · codebase validation across
1M+ token windows.

RECONCILE NOTE (SDD-993, 2026-07-13): cross-GPU tensor-parallel spans the
two INTERNAL cards — RTX PRO 6000 96 GB (primary) + RTX 5090 32 GB
(secondary) — which run x8/x8 and can P2P. The RTX 4090 is an OcuLink eGPU
(PCIe 4.0 x4): fine for VRAM-resident inference, but do NOT tensor-parallel
across it (the x4 eGPU link is bandwidth-bound). DeepSeek-V3-Quant (120 GB)
is the large-oracle placement on the PRO 6000. See docs/sdd/993-*.md.

**Hardware-profile compatibility:** sain-01

**Trinity allocations:**

| Agent | Tier | Target | Core mask | Engine | Model |
|-------|------|--------|-----------|--------|-------|
| `synthesizer_01` | oracle | cuda:0,cuda:1 | `—` | `vllm` | `DeepSeek-V3-Quant` |

**GPU state:**

- Primary: `full-power` (invocation: `nvidia-smi -i 0 -pm 1 -lgc 1410,1965`)
- Secondary: `full-power` (invocation: `nvidia-smi -i 1 -pm 1`)

**Expected power draw (W):**

- cpu: 80
- gpu_primary: 300
- gpu_secondary: 350
- gpu_egpu: 350
- system_total: ~1080

**Observability:**

- Primary metric: `sovereign_os_inference_route_total{tier="oracle"}`
